Description: The plot frame that is used for editing the form.
Defines the form as being static or dynamic: 0 = static, 1 = dynamic.
In this form property, you define the properties that cause a new data header area to be generated on the report page when they change.
Maximum number of columns to be generated on a page.
Maximum number of rows to be generated on a page.
Width of the individual columns on a form.
Height of the individual rows on a form.

Sequence in which rows and columns are to be filled out: 0 = By row or 1 = By column. "By row" means that all the cells of a row are populated first before switching to the next row. In this case, jumpers are displayed vertically. "By column" means that all cells of a column are populated first before switching to the next column. In this case, jumpers are displayed horizontally.

If indirect properties are indexed with 0, then the values for all (meaningful) indices are printed together. This is generally done using one-row lists separated by semicolons. In forms, however, you can output these values as separate elements (rows/columns).
Height of a first level jumper in the terminal diagram.

Used in forms for representing the terminal diagram. The assignment of a connection to two connection points is thus shown as a three-tuple (an ordered quantity of assignments); multiple connection assignments are each separated by a "pipe" character.
Clearance between two jumpers at neighboring levels.

If jumpers are shown graphically in terminal diagrams, a small filled circle is set on the base line on each leg of the jumper. The radius of this circle is specified with this property (in software coordinates).

Nodes of jumpers are graphically shown in the terminal diagram with a small filled circle. The radius of this circle is specified with this property (in software coordinates). (Start and end "legs" do not have dots.)

Designation: Jumpers: Height of internal wire jumper / insertable jumper
Description: Height of an internal wire jumper or insertable jumper of the first level in the terminal diagram. If the property is not set, the "Jumpers: Height of first level" property is used instead.

Designation: Jumpers: Height of external wire jumper/ insertable jumper
Description: Height of an external wire jumper or insertable jumper of the first level in the terminal diagram. If the property is not set, the "Jumpers: Height of first level" property is used instead.

Designation: Jumpers: Spacing of internal wire jumper/ insertable jumper
Description: Clearance between two internal wire jumpers or insertable jumpers at neighboring levels. If the property is not set, the "Jumpers: Height of first level" property is used instead.

Designation: Jumpers: Spacing of external wire jumper/ insertable jumper
Description: Clearance between two external wire jumpers or insertable jumpers at neighboring levels. If the property is not set, the "Jumpers: Height of first level" property is used instead.
Format for automatically assigned page descriptions in the generation of reporting lists.

A fixed graphic can be inserted in connection diagrams or other forms using the "Graphic" placeholder. The configuration property exists for the placeholder to allow the graphic to be exchanged with any other graphic. Here you assign an index value to the graphic. The graphic is then selected in the "Graphic" placeholder by specifying the index value.
Assignment of a function definition to a symbol variant. Used when generating a page in order to create a fixed symbol graphic.
Assignment of a property and / or a value to a symbol variant. This property contains the assignment table assigning a graphic to the property and / or the value. A max. of 10 assignments allowed.
Graphical display of jumpers: 0 = Line or 1 = Angle.
Line thickness used for displaying jumpers.
Number of all jumper levels.

Designation: Cable chart: No. of cables A
Description: Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position A).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area A
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position A).

Designation: Cable chart: No. of cables B
Description: Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position B).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area B
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position B).

Designation: Cable chart: No. of cables C
Description: Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position C).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area C
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position C).

Designation: Cable chart: No. of cables D
Description: Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position D).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area D
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for terminal connection points with the form position D).
Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for internal terminal connection points).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area, internal
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for internal terminal connection points).
Maximum number of columns or rows (dependent on the form orientation) that can be displayed in the cable chart (for external terminal connection points).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for data area, external
Description: Distance to be displayed for the data area of the cable chart (for external terminal connection points).
Distance to be displayed between neighboring symbols.
Number of neighboring symbols to be displayed.
Specifies whether multi-level terminals are displayed next to each other or under each other; useful for terminal line-up diagrams.
Selection of structure identifiers to be displayed in the form:

Designation: Summarize external and internal targets in internal targets
Description: If this property is activated in a form for a device connection diagram, then external and internal targets are output together as internal targets.

Designation: Use 'Plot frame to edit form' property for reports
Description: Specifies whether the "Use 'Plot frame to edit form' property for reports" property is used or not.

Shows the form for the following page, which can also have its own plot frame. The form for the following page is used with its plot frame as of the second page of a reporting block. If another next page form has been entered for the next page form, this is not taken into account when generating reports.

If several values are output in a text, then the character entered here is used as a separator. (A character string or a line break can also be entered as a separator.) If the property remains empty then a semicolon is used as a separator. Example: A1;A3;A6.

Designation: Generate data headers for the first x sorting properties
Description: This property specifies whether and which sorting properties are to be used when generating a new data header area:
Monitors the report action of the manufacturer/supplier list:

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header A
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for terminal connections with the form position A).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header B
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for terminal connections with the form position B).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header C
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for terminal connections with the form position C).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header D
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for terminal connections with the form position D).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header, internal
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for internal terminal connections).

Designation: Cable chart: Spacing for header, external
Description: Distance to be displayed for the header of the cable chart (for external terminal connections).

Designation: Cable chart: Header perpendicular to data area
Description: If this property is activated, the header of the cable chart is displayed across the data area. If it is not activated, the header has the same setup as the data area.

Designation: Connection diagram: Number of steps, internal
Description: For connection point diagrams with variable levels, this specifies how many levels are planned for displaying internal targets.

Designation: Connection diagram: Interval between steps, internal
Description: For connection point diagrams with variable levels, this specifies the spacing between two levels when displaying internal targets. All levels are displayed with the same spacing.

Designation: Connection diagram: Number of steps, external
Description: For connection point diagrams with variable levels, this specifies how many levels are planned for displaying external targets.

Designation: Connection diagram: Interval between steps, external
Description: For connection point diagrams with variable levels, this specifies the spacing between two levels when displaying external targets. All levels are displayed with the same spacing.
There can be blank data rows in reports when the output properties are blank. Blank data lines are not output if this property is selected.

When several values with numbers are entered at the end of a text, the character (or character sequence) entered here is used as a separator. If the property remains blank, the character sequence "..." is used as a separator. Example: A1...A5 or A1-A5.
The header is repeated by default for every device when reporting dynamic forms. If this property is activated, the header is also repeated in each new column of the report.

If this property is activated, all targets will be output up to the first level in the connection diagram. For all other levels, only the first targets are output. In the connection diagram, the targets are placed in a row and are only entered when the connection points belong to the same device and are next to each other in the graphic. In case of several targets, the connection graphic is represented as an oblique line.

Designation: Connection diagram: Output only connected connection points at targets
Description: This property stands for device, terminal, cable as well as pin-connection diagrams. If it is activated, only the connected connection points of the displayed targets are output. This helps to save space in the connection diagrams. This property only relates to targets and does not affect the devices, cables, terminal strips, or plugs to be evaluated. These functions are also listed in the connection diagrams. Only the connected function connection points are output at the terminal strip, e.g. in a terminal connection diagram for a motor which is the target of a terminal strip.

Designation: Connection diagram: Internal targets in table form
Description: If this form property is enabled, the properties of the internal targets are displayed in table format (and not grouped) in connection diagrams. A separate row is displayed for every target. If the internal and external targets are displayed in table format, the column width of the form is not shrunk to the report page.

Designation: Connection diagram: External targets in table form
Description: If this form property is enabled, the properties of the external targets are displayed in table format (and not grouped) in connection diagrams. A separate row is displayed for every target. If the internal and external targets are displayed in table format, the column width of the form is not shrunk to the report page.

If this form property is enabled, deleted pages are output in the revision overview. The option "Pages" must also be selected for the "Revision output type" property (ID 13106). Deleted pages are not output when the property is not enabled.

Designation: Connection diagram: Do not divide targets
Description: If this property is activated, targets in connection point diagrams are always represented completely on a report page, rather than distributed on several report pages. Terminal strips and plugs are always separated regardless of this setting, if necessary, and the separation is drawn with a dotted line.

Designation: Jumpers: Suppress graphic for saddle jumper option

Description: Adjusts the row height in dynamic forms. If a cell contains longer text that causes a line break, then only this row automatically becomes higher.

Designation: Use new column for each device
Description: In dynamically summarized reports, a new device (e.g. terminal strip or plug) begins in a new column.
Enables/disables the display of accessories that can be aligned in reports.

Designation: No line feed in data header area
Description: Controls the position of the data header area in forms. If the property is activated then the data header area is output with the next data row. If the property is deactivated then the data header area is output in its own row.
This form property shows which revision data are output in the revision overview:

This property is only available in summarized parts lists. If it is activated, then a text - not a value - from the project settings will be output for the corresponding property in functions that have a variable defined in the placeholder object.
Specifies which symbol variant is displayed for output in the form for symbols that are represented by the "Symbol graphic" property.

Designation: Reuse data header area for new column
Description: The data header area is repeated by default for every function for reporting dynamic forms. If this property is enabled, the data header area is also repeated in every new column of the report.

You specify the form properties for which identical data are to be entered in a row. For instance, you can combine the data for each page type in the table of contents. A semi-colon and the character sequence "..." are used as separators by default for entered values. However, you can also specify other separators via the properties "Separator for multiple values" and "Separator for combined values".

Designation: Align graphics to insertion point (without alignment box)
Description: The property can be used in forms and does affect all placeholders for graphics for which the alignment box has not been activated. The handle is set at the insertion point of the placeholder text for the symbols and macros inserted via this type of placeholder.

If this property is activated, a row is generated in terminal diagrams and terminal connection diagrams for each pair of connection points. Terminals with more than two connection points are automatically split across multiple rows. In addition, terminal connection points that are not connected are output. In addition, jumpers between an internal and an external connection point are not evaluated and displayed as jumpers.

If this property is activated, only the symbols actually used in the project will be output in the symbol overview. If the property is deactivated, all symbols of the symbol libraries stored in the project will be output.

Designation: Alphanumeric sorting for internal connection points
Description: This form property is intended for use in table device connection diagrams in order to output the connection points there in an alphanumeric, collated, manner. For all connection points to be output internally and collated jointly, you must also activate the "Summarize external and internal targets in internal targets" (ID 13054) property. You cannot collate only external connection points.

Designation: Connection diagram: Target tracking across all devices
Description: If this property is activated, the target tracking in connection point diagrams will be continued via any devices. For the search for further targets, the Target tracking (PLC) to property is used in the connection point logic of the devices. In addition, the Connection point diagram: Output only connected connection points (ID 13085) property is evaluated as activated.

Designation: Apply abbreviation rules to all structure properties

Description: When this form property is activated, all the subordinate segments are also determined and output for each segment in the data area of a report of the type "Structure segment plan" or "Planning object plan".

This property is only available for forms of the "Symbol overview" type. If the property is activated, only the symbol graphics are output in the symbol overview, but not the properties displayed by default at the symbol.

Designation: Connection diagram: Output only connected connection points
Description: This property is only available for device connection diagrams. if it is activated, only the connected connection points of the reported device are displayed.
